response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong software engineer with broad full-stack and high-performance systems experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched with this role. His primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang web and backend development; C++ appears only briefly at National Instruments (~2014–2015) and in one academic project. The role demands deep, current C++ proficiency as its core requirement, alongside industrial automation domain knowledge, MQTT/industrial-protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), and familiarity with IEC 62443 secure-development standards — none of which appear in the resume.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance and low-latency systems work (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby: async I/O, fibers, millions of HTTP req/s)
Solid event-driven architecture and distributed-systems background across multiple roles
Consistent agile engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code-quality advocacy
concerns: C++ is effectively dormant — last substantive use was 2014–2015; current primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
Zero industrial automation, IoT, or industrial-protocol experience (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT not mentioned)
No evidence of secure-development lifecycle, IEC 62443, or any cybersecurity/SDL practices
